As we can see, the expenses of Branch 1 are mostly divided into Sales and HR departments, and to a lesser extent also into Customer Service, Operations, and Finance. Another example would be Branch 3, whose expenses mainly come from the Operations and Marketing departments, but also from the Customer Service and Sales departments in a lower percentage.

 

Step 3: Adding a script: an advanced version  

If you seek to bring a touch of colour to your chart, you just need to add the following line to the previous code (highlighted in green):

 

 

library('plotly')

plot_ly(

  type = "sankey",

  orientation = "h",

  node = list(

    label = c("Marketing", "Sales", "HR", "Finance", "Operations", "Customer Service", "Branch 1", "Branch 2", "Branch 3", "Branch 4", "Branch 5", "Branch 7", "Branch 6"),

    color = c("green", "yellow", "blue", "orange", "red", "black", "pink", "gold", "violet", "magenta", "purple", "lime", "cyan", "silver"),

    pad = 15,

    thickness = 20,

    line = list(

      color = "black",

      width = 0.5

    )

  ),

  link = list(

    source = c(0,0,0,1,1,1,2,2,3,3,4,4,4,5,5,5,5,5),

    target = c(9,8,7,8,7,6,7,6,7,6,8,7,6,12,10,8,7,6),

    value =  c(61.5,34.8,3.46,1.42,8.6,89.7,87.6,12.1,94.6,5.20,96,2.30,1.69,63.1,8.7,15.8,2.81,8.83),

    color = c("blue", "rgba(255,255,0,0.7)", "rgba(0,0,255,0.7)", "rgba(255,165,0,0.7)", "rgba(255,0,0,0.7)", "rgba(0,0,0,0.7)", "rgba(255,192,203,0.7)", "rgba(255,215,0,0.7)", "rgba(238,130,238,0.7)", "rgba(255,0,255,0.7)", "rgba(128,0,128,0.7)", "rgba(0,255,0,0.7)", "rgba(0,255,255,0.7)", "rgba(192,192,192,0.7)")

  )

)

 

 

In this case, the colour codes are just for example purposes, so feel free to change them for whatever colour you prefer for your chart. Once more, if the Preview lane shows a chart result, click Apply and the Sankey chart will be displayed, as below:

In this last example, the results are the same but the colours help much more to recognise more quickly the percentage of expenses of each department for each branch. For example, the two main big spenders in Branch 2 come from the Finance and HR departments. However, it also has expenses from the Sales department, and at a much lower percentage, from the Operations, Marketing and Customer Service departments. However, Branches 5 and 6 only have one department as an expense: Customer Service. Branch 4 would also only have expenses from the Marketing department.

 

Step 4: Making your Sankey chart dynamic

Last but not least, you can make your Sankey chart dynamic. This way, if your dataset is modified, your chart will be too! To do so, don’t forget to assign your input data, select rows and columns, add filters (if needed), and edit the script!

As a conclusion, the Sankey chart proves to be a valuable asset, allowing organisations to boost data relationships and insights. In a world where data speaks loud, SAP Analytics Cloud, coupled with the dynamic capabilities of Sankey charts, empowers businesses to navigate complex information and make decisions that drive success.

Happy charting!

 

---

You can also read:

https://blogs.sap.com/2019/10/28/adding-an-interactive-sankey-chart-to-sap-analytics-cloud/

https://blogs.sap.com/2019/08/01/hands-on-tutorial-create-r-visualizations-in-the-sap-analytics-cloud/

entry.title: Real time implementation of Scripting to create, update and delete master dimensions in SAC
entry.content:
Introduction

Welcome fellow SAC planning technical analysts! If you’ve ever been sifting through numerous blogs and forums to find comprehensive content on SAC scripting, you’re not alone. The challenge is real, and even ChatGPT has limitations due to the SAC API calls' specific nature. In this blog, I aim to bridge that gap by providing a detailed, real-time guide on creating, updating, and deleting master data in SAC master dimensions using scripting.

Why Scripting in SAC?

Scripting in SAC offers a powerful way to automate and enhance your planning processes. Whether you're dealing with dynamic master data updates or managing complex planning scenarios, scripting provides the flexibility and control you need.

Getting Started

Before diving into the code, let’s outline what we’ll cover:

  1. Creating Members
  2. Updating Members
  3. Deleting Members

To get an Overview of these Functions, you can visit this blog for more information:-
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/a-scripting-guide-for-master-data-crud-operations-in-sap-analytics-cloud/ba-p/13571806

We’ll also look at how to publish and revert changes in your planning model. Let's make scripting in SAC not just functional but fun and easy to grasp!

Publishing and Reverting Changes
After making changes to your master data, it's crucial to publish or revert those changes to reflect in your public version.
Publish Data:
Table_name.getPlanning().getPublicVersion("VersionName").publish();
console.log("Data published successfully!");
Revert Data:
Table_name.getPlanning().getPublicVersion("VersionName").revert();
console.log("Data reverted successfully!");

Real-Time Implementation

I’ve implemented this code in a real-time scenario, and it works like a charm! Here is a glimpse of how the Code works with different buttons to perform each of the Tasks. 


To perform this, We have incorporated three buttons each to handle a specific task of Creating, updating and deleting dimension members. On selection of any of these buttons, a Popup appears which allows to user to update Master dimensions. 

Each of the popups consists of the usage of the Planning function to create, update, and delete dimensional data. I will discuss the coding below in the following screenshots on how it is configured for each of the scenarios above. 




Conclusion

Mastering the creation, updating, and deletion of members in SAC in Real-time through scripting can significantly enhance your data management efficiency. By leveraging these scripts, you can automate routine tasks, maintain data integrity, and ensure your planning models are always up to date. I hope this guide helps you understand the implementation of the scripting of CRUD operations in SAC to enhance your planning requirements.

Stay tuned for more insights and happy scripting!

Feel free to share your thoughts and questions in the comments below!
SAP Analytics Cloud for planning SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: Story using Tab Strips in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
As a storyteller (developer) in SAP analytics Cloud we always experience this challenge of too many pages in the story or a “Performance Optimization Tip – too many widgets. Remove a few too better performance”. And then the conundrum starts “Shall I keep all or remove some or how best to utilize the space”

One solution which can help in few use cases is Tab strips. Let me give you an example

Use Case:

Superstore Model- Charts and Tables are based on Ship Mode.

Requirement is to see the charts and table filtered on each mode in separate pages. Replicating charts and tables along with different widgets in each page will eat up space and affect performance.

We can repurpose the Charts and Tables in main page in all the tabs.

Steps

  1. Open a blank canvas
  2. Add Tab strips 

 

  1. Rename the Tabs in builder panel

 

  1. Add couple of Charts and table in first tab (Summary)

 

  1. You can rename the Charts and Tables as per your preference under outline

 

 

  1. Click of FX in Tabstrip and write the below code

Save and view.

The idea is tab name acts as a filter and when you click on the tab it will move the charts and table from the Summary page after filtering it.

Few things to keep in mind.

  • Always use the id instead of description
  • When selecting filter in Set Dimension Filter, use “Open member Selector”.

Hope this helps. Let me know your thoughts.

SAP Analytics Cloud SAP Analytics Cloud for planning SAP Analytics Cloud, augmented analytics

entry.title: Creating a story with Dynamic Version & Date Combinations
entry.content:
Introduction

 

Designing a story in SAP Analytics Cloud can be easy and straightforward with many features to create calculations, columns, styles. The drag and drop functionality helps users create reports with different layouts of various complexity . Optimized story design is also helpful to create advanced stories and reporting layouts using JavaScript to achieve complex functionality and user interactions.

When designing any report or input form, if the layout contains Version and Date dimensions, adding multiple members of versions and Months or Years can result in a Layout that has columns with all the combinations of the members. For example, if the selections contain Actual, Budget for Version and Date contains years 2024 and 2025, the report will have 4 columns. Even though the intention is to show only 2 columns with combinations of [Actual, 2023] and [Budget, 2024], the final report contains all 4 combinations.

There are ways in which we can show only valid/required combinations, for example hiding unwanted columns or suppress columns with no data or using Advanced Filters. But these approaches are not dynamic. Every time there is a change in Version or Date, the columns need to be adjusted manually. It might also create many static reports to achieve different combination reports.

The alternate approach is to create dynamic reports is using Optimized story design using JavaScript and SAC Advanced Script API to show columns with only valid/required combinations. This blog provides required design and logic required for interactions and filtering.
 

Step 1 - Creating Design Elements

The design of the dashboard uses dropdown menus instead of filters define within Story. The user can select required Dimension members from dropdown and on submit the selections will be sent to the table filters dynamically. We will use story calculations to create required combinations and further use SAC Analytics designer and API to update dimension filters with required combinations.

Step 1-a
  • Create a New story using Optimized Story design.
  • Add 4 Dropdown menus ( Insert -> Filters/Controls -> Dropdown). 2 for Actual / 2023 and 2 for Budget/ 2024. 
  • Dropdown names dd_fy1, dd_fy2 for years & dd_cat1, dd_cat2 for versions. 
  • Add 1 submit button ( Insert -> Button )
  • Button name : btn_1
  • Add 1 Table and connect to model. Add necessary filters. ( We need to update the filters at a later stage to show necessary columns)
  • Table Name: table_1

 

Step 1-b
Once the dropdown menus are added, from the script edit page, add the values required for the dropdown menus. Year dropdown has values as Years and Version dropdown has values as Version names. Below images show the options used for each dropdown.
 
 
 
 
The final result will be 4 dropdown menus with values and a Submit button. 
 

 

Step 2 - Logic 

Till now we have added design elements in the Story. Now we need to add logic to capture the values selected from dropdown menus and send them story filters.

logic to be added on btn_1 onClick function

The logic has 3 main parts. 

  1. extract the selections used from the dropdown menus and pass them to variables and arrays
  2. create valid combinations by combining Version + year values and push into array
  3. Filter the Version, Year and Valid Combinations values on the table

 

 

 

 

//extract selected versions into variables 
var version1 = dd_cat1.getSelectedKey(); 
var version2 = dd_cat2.getSelectedKey(); 

// append public to variables created above 
var col1_version = "public."+version1; 
var col2_version = "public."+version2; 

//extract selected versions into variables 
var col1_fy = dd_fy1.getSelectedKey(); 
var col2_fy = dd_fy2.getSelectedKey(); 

// save table into variable 
var ds_table1 = table_1.getDataSource(); 

// get dimension names into variables. Note the order of dimensions added in columns. 
// 0 is version, 1 is for year 
var version_col =table_1.getDimensionsOnColumns()[0]; 
var fy_col =table_1.getDimensionsOnColumns()[1]; 

// create an empty array to hold version values and add the version variables created above 
var version_cols_values = ArrayUtils.create(Type.string); 
version_cols_values.push(col1_version); 
version_cols_values.push(col2_version); 

// create an empty array to hold year values and add the year variables created above 
var fy_cols_values = ArrayUtils.create(Type.string); 
fy_cols_values.push(col1_fy); 
fy_cols_values.push(col2_fy); 

// create an empty array for valid combinations 
// for example Actual2023, Budget2024 
var valid_comb = ArrayUtils.create(Type.string); 
valid_comb.push(version1+col1_fy); 
valid_comb.push(version2+col2_fy); 


// set the 3 dimension filters for Version, Date, valid_combinations on the table 
ds_table1.setDimensionFilter(version_col,version_cols_values); 
ds_table1.setDimensionFilter(fy_col,fy_cols_values); 

ds_table1.setDimensionFilter(table_1.getDimensionsOnColumns()[2],valid_comb);

 

 

 

 

Step 3 - Story

The JavaScript logic above filters the dimension values for Version and Time , Fiscal Year values. But filtering alone on Version and Time still result in additional columns which are not required. So we need to create an additional calculated dimension call "valid_combinations" to identify the required columns and filter them during the runtime.

 

 

Below is the code used for creating the calculated dimension. The code uses Version and Fiscal Year to derive the all the possible combinations of columns. One can add many versions, years and create all past and future values possible.

 

 

 

 

IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Actual" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2022"),
        "Actual2022",
        IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Actual" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2023"),
                "Actual2023",
                IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Actual" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2024"),
                        "Actual2024",
                        IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Actual" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2025"),
                                "Actual2025",
                                IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Budget" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2022"),
                                        "Budget2022",
                                        IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Budget" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2023"),
                                                "Budget2023",
                                                IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Budget" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2024"),
                                                        "Budget2024",
                                                        IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Budget" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2025"),
                                                                "Budget2025",
                                                                IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Plan" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2022"),
                                                                        "Plan2022",
                                                                        IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Plan" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2023"),
                                                                                "Plan2023",
                                                                                IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Plan" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2024"),
                                                                                        "Plan2024",
                                                                                        IF(([d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Version].[p/ID]="public.Plan" and [d/"PLANNING_MODEL_SAC":Time].[p/FISCAL_YEAR]="2025"),
                                                                                                "Plan2025",
                                                                                                "Actual2025"
                                                                                        )
                                                                                )
                                                                        )
                                                                )
                                                        )
                                                )
                                        )
                                )
                        )
                )
        )
)

 

 

 

 

Result

The final result will contain table with only required columns.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Release 2024.13
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud brings together analytics and planning with unique integration to SAP applications and seamless access to heterogenous data sources. It helps everyone in your organization make decisions without doubt with trusted insights and integrated plans.

This blog post introduces the features of the SAP Analytics Cloud 2024.13 release available for SAP Analytics Cloud FastTrack customers. For SAP Analytics Cloud Quarterly Release Cycle (QRC) customers, this release and its features will be available as part of the QRC Q3 2024 release. View the QRC release schedule here.

 

System Administration and Technical Requirements

Although not required, you may upgrade to the latest SAP Analytics Cloud agent version 1.0.409 to take advantage of all data acquisition types.

The agent’s minimum version remains at 1.0.75, with the following exceptions:

  • Version 1.0.99 for SAP ERP/SQL Database
  • Version 1.0.365 for SAP universe (UNX). The SAP Analytics Cloud agent requires additional setup steps, for more details see SAP Note 3262098
  • Version 1.0.353 for SAP Business Warehouse (BW)
  • Version 1.0.235 for SAP HANA View
  • Version 1.0.248 for File Server

For more information, see System Requirements and Technical Prerequisites.

Regarding supported features and required updates for the integration of SAP BW with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2541557 for further details.

Regarding supported features and required updates for live universe access through SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2771921 for further details.

Please download the latest version of the Simple Deployment Kit from Software Center.

Note: To avoid downloading older versions and potentially exposing users to a vulnerable bundling of the Cloud Connector with SAPJVM, or Apache Tomcat, only the three latest versions of the kits are available to download.

For Cloud agent version change management details, refer to SAP Note 3264839.

 

Release Highlights

  • Administration and Service Management
    • Data Audit: Ability to delete audit data based on other filters | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Disable the welcome email for new users | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • AI for Planning and Analytics
    • Just Ask Comparison | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Data Exploration
    • Data Analyzer - Share an insight from within the tool
    • Data Analyzer - Shortcut Link | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Calculations Dialog: LOG, LOG10, CEIL, ROUND, TRUNC
    • Context Menu Enhancements in Builder Panel / Available Objects list
    • More Filter Members Capabilities (Filter with Swap/Remove; Contextual Highlighting for Tuple Filter) | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Enterprise Planning
    • Data Actions - Mapping Strategy "Property" | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Multi Actions – creation and update by BI users | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Microsoft Office Integration
    • Frontend Calculations referencing members
    • Null Suppression
  • Story Design
    • Smart Grouping in Charts in the optimized story experience
    • Public URL API support for BW Hierarchies
  • Story Extensibility
    • Widget Customization Add-on to support legend | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Create calculated or account-based dimensions in custom widgets as charts and tables | (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Administration and Service Management

 

Data Audit: Ability to delete audit data based on other filters

When deleting data changes to reduce the size of your data change log, you can now add filters on dimensions of the model, as well as Audit Time, Audit User, and Audit Action, to control which data changes to delete. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Disable the welcome email for new users

You can now disable the welcome email from being sent to new users added to you tenant. For more information, see Manage the Welcome Email Notification. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

AI for Planning and Analytics

 

Just Ask Comparison

You can now use the following in Just Ask questions: (SAP Road Map Explorer)

  • Compare two measures: Compare sales with expenses
  • Compare two measures and a dimension: Compare sales with expenses by location
  • Time comparisons: Compare sales with expenses by location in current year
  • Combine a comparison and a calculation: Compare average sales by location with average expenses by location this year
  • Combine a version dimension with a measure comparison: Compare actual sales with forecast in Canada

 

Data Exploration

 

Data Analyzer - Share an insight from within the tool

You can now share an insight right from data analyzer by using the function Share Insight in the new Share toolbar button. In the Share Insight dialog you can add the users or teams with whom you’d like to share the insight, set the access type and set if new recipients should get an email.

 
Data Analyzer - Shortcut Link

If you want to capture the state of the current view on your data in data analyzer without saving it, you can now create a shortcut link by using the function Create Shortcut Link in the new Share button in the in the toolbar. The generated shortcut link can be copied to the clipboard and thus be added for example to an email or opened in a new tab. A cleanup task is scheduled in the system configuration area to delete shortcut links in recurrent period. As an administrator you can reconfigure the default period. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Calculations Dialog: LOG, LOG10, CEIL, ROUND, TRUNC

We have introduced support for two mathematical and three conversion functions:

  • LOG(number) and LOG10(number) compute the natural logarithm and the base-10 logarithm of a positive number, respectively.
  • CEIL returns a real number that is greater or equal to the entered number
  • ROUND rounds to a specified number of decimal places.
  • TRUNC truncates the provided number at the specified decimal place.

These new functions enhance data manipulation in both BW and HANA systems, allowing for more precise and advanced arrangements of data. 

Categorization of functions:

  • Functions are now grouped under the following categories - Conversion, Logical, Date and Time, Mathematical, Lookup & Reference, Business, and Dynamic Time. 

  • Added a new search box in the function's sections for quicker access to functions, maintaining category clarity during search.

The new categorized layout with search capabilities enhances user experience and streamlines the generation of calculations.

 

Context Menu Enhancements in Builder Panel / Available Objects list

In data analyzer multiple enhancements have been added to improve the user workflow:

  • Added splitter and the Expand/Collapse button ensure a better space management in the data analyzer Builder panel.
  • In the Available Objects list you can now use the More menu with filter functions.
  • In the Available Objects list there you can now work with a context menu by using the right-mouse click function.
  • In the Available Objects list now all members of the accounts/measures are listed, not only the booked

 

More Filter Members Capabilities (Filter with Swap/Remove; Contextual Highlighting for Tuple Filter)

Table context menu enhanced with extra filter options: (SAP Road Map Explorer)

Filter Member and >

  • Swap Dimension With > then this context menu offers the user to Apply Filter members with this selection and Swap with another dimension to be placed on Axis
  • Remove Dimension{}then this context menu offers the user to apply Filter members with this selection and remove the dimension(s) from Axis.
  • Filter color Highlighter - The color highlighter with different filter options (Filter Member, Filter, Exclude Member, Exclude, Filter Members)

 

Enterprise Planning

 

Data Actions - Mapping Strategy "Property"

In the cross model copy step one can now select the property which is used for the Identical names mapping. With this feature the user can map automatically dimensions by maintaining an additional property in the modeler

 

Multi Actions – creation and update by BI users

The standard "BI Admin" role contains multi actions privileges. Users with a BI license have the capability to create and execute multi actions that include BI steps only (excluding BI embedded license). Additionally, the names of the action triggers, such as multi action trigger, data action trigger, and BPC planning sequence trigger, have changed and they are now called starters: multi action starter, data action starter, and BPC planning sequence starter.

 

Microsoft Office Integration

 

Frontend Calculations referencing members

In your front-end calculations, you can now reference dimension members and properties.

This enables you to combine SAP Analytics Cloud and Excel features as you can use XLOOKUP, IF, and more Excel features in your formulas.

This feature is available to customers as of June 19, 2024.

 

Null Suppression

You now have the option Null Suppression to suppress null values (-) in a table. The option is available in the context menu on Rows or Columns.

With the existing option Zero Suppression, you can suppress zero (0) and null (-) values in one step.

This feature is available to customers as of June 19, 2024.

 

Story Design

 

Smart Grouping in Charts in the optimized story experience

You can now apply Smart Grouping to Charts in the optimized story experience.

 
Public URL API support for BW Hierarchies

The SAP Analytics Cloud URL API allows you to filter on time and Parent child Hierarchies which are not default for BW Live Data connections. fXXHierarchy  parameter supports to filter on time and parent-child hierarchies with different hierarchal values.  

This functionality allows developers to generate URLs for stories with embedded hierarchy node filters, enhancing the end-user experience. Users will benefit from pre-filtered stories that present data specific to particular hierarchy nodes.

Parameter Syntax: f<XX>Hierarchy=<HierarchyName>

  • <XX> is any number between 01 and 99. If there are several filters passed for filtering then assign a unique value of <XX> to each filter. <HierarchyName> is the name of the hierarchy you want to filter on.

This is only supported in the Optimized Story Experience.

 

Story Extensibility

 

Widget Customization Add-on to support legend

Users can now develop their own widget add-ons for customizing chart legends. This new capability enhances the overall storytelling experience of data representation. Supported chart types are bar/column, stacked bar/column, stacked area and line. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

Refer to SAP Analytics Cloud Widget Add-On Developer Guide for more.

 

Create calculated or account-based dimensions in custom widgets as charts and tables

In the optimized story experience, you can create and utilize calculated or account-based dimensions for custom widgets. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

This blog post only provides a high-level overview of the latest features and enhancements of SAP Analytics Cloud. You can find more details on these features in your In-App Help Guide for FastTrack customers and see this SAP Knowledge Base Article if you’d like to learn more about FastTrack and Quarterly Release Cycle releases.

If you have any questions regarding the content in this blog post, you can comment below. If you have product specific or technical questions, post a question to our SAP Analytics Cloud Questions & Answers forum. Visit our SAP Analytics Cloud Community pages to find more product information, best practices, and more. And check out our SAP Road Map Explorer to see more upcoming features of SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: Unlock the Full Potential of SAP Datasphere: Your Essential Guide from SAP PRESS
entry.content:
The Author of this Book

Hello SAP Professionals,

I’m Geetha Madhuri Bobbili, an SAP Data and Analytics Senior Manager at Deloitte, where I lead initiatives leveraging SAP technologies to drive innovation and deliver impactful solutions to our esteemed clients. I’m thrilled to introduce my latest SAP PRESS publication, “Introducing SAP Datasphere”. This comprehensive resource offers insights into the functionalities and capabilities of SAP Datasphere, empowering you to navigate its complexities with confidence.

Through my experience, I've witnessed how SAP Datasphere revolutionizes data management by simplifying integration and preserving crucial business context—something often overlooked in traditional processes. One notable project with a global insurance company was a significant milestone for me. It highlighted SAP Datasphere's potential to empower organizations to become truly data-driven.

This journey has inspired me to delve deeper into this technology, explore its full capabilities, and share my insights with a broader audience. Writing this book is my way of giving back to the SAP community that has given me so much.

Bridging the Knowledge Gap

I wrote this book to fill a gap in the available learning materials for SAP Datasphere. Recognizing the need for practical insights and real-world scenarios, my goal is to empower SAP professionals with the knowledge to fully understand and utilize SAP Datasphere in their organizations. Through this book, I aim to equip professionals to excel in their roles and drive innovation.

What is SAP Datasphere

In today’s digital era, seamless access to critical business data is essential for any organization's transformation. Managing data across on-premise and cloud systems can be complex, requiring significant effort in extraction, rebuilding, and integration. Maintaining business context during data transfers to cloud data lakes is crucial due to their intricate structures.

SAP Datasphere, powered by SAP HANA Cloud, addresses these challenges by enabling rapid data movement while preserving essential business context through real-time and batch replications. It offers a unified platform with a revolutionary business data fabric architecture that consolidates critical data from various sources, simplifying decision-making processes and ensuring secure and scalable access to vital business data.

As a cornerstone in modern data management, SAP Datasphere enhances secure data handling, fosters innovation, and facilitates real-time connectivity and integration across systems, providing a unified view of an organization’s data.

What You’ll Discover

In this book, you’ll gain a thorough understanding of SAP Datasphere’s core components, data warehousing strategy, integration capabilities, and data management techniques. Additionally, you’ll learn how to acquire, secure, prepare, and model data effectively through real-world scenarios, and consume data for analysis and reporting. Each section of the book is meticulously crafted to provide valuable insights and practical guidance while adhering to the page limit set by SAP PRESS (approximately 125 - 130 pages). I have ensured comprehensive coverage of each feature with detailed, step-by-step explanations.

Contents at a Glance

Chapter 1: Understanding SAP Datasphere

This chapter sets the foundation for the rest of the book by providing an overview of SAP Datasphere's capabilities and its potential business benefits. You'll explore the core components of SAP Datasphere, understand SAP's data warehousing strategy, and learn how to navigate the SAP Datasphere interface.

Chapter 2: Exploring SAP Datasphere Spaces

In this chapter, you'll dive into space management in SAP Datasphere. Learn how to create spaces, assign disk and in-memory storage, set priorities, and manage resources. The chapter explains how to monitor storage usage within spaces to ensure resource optimization and organize your data landscape effectively.

Chapter 3: Exploring SAP Datasphere Integration Options

This chapter describes the integration capabilities of SAP Datasphere. Discover how the platform simplifies data integration across various sources, including cloud, on-premise, SAP, non-SAP sources, and partner tools. Explore the process of creating, modifying, and verifying connections, as well as managing real-time replication for optimized data flow. By the end of this chapter, you'll have a comprehensive understanding of SAP Datasphere's integration options and their application in real-world scenarios.

Chapter 4: Acquiring Data with the Data Builder

Learn to import data from different sources, create and modify tables, and work with data flows in this chapter. The chapter also covers using replication flows for efficient data copying and task chains for process automation.

Chapter 5: Data Preparation in the Data Builder

This chapter covers enabling currency conversion using TCUR* tables and views in SAP Datasphere. Discover how to share currency conversion tables across spaces. Explore combining, filtering, and enriching data using graphical and SQL views, as well as persisting view data for performance optimization. Learn about intelligent lookup for joining entities when traditional methods are impractical and creating entity-relationship (E/R) models.

Chapter 6: Securing Data with Data Access Controls

Introducing data access controls in SAP Datasphere for ensuring data security. Learn how to apply these controls to views or objects, restricting access based on specific rules. Explore creating and configuring data access controls, defining user criteria with permissions, and enforcing these criteria across multiple views.

Chapter 7: Data Modeling in the Data Builder

Enhance data entities with semantic information for broader accessibility in this chapter. Create fact entities for numerical analysis, dimension entities for data categorization, and text entities for multilingual translation. Focus on analytic models, crucial for data consumption in SAP Analytics Cloud, covering predefined measures, hierarchies, filters, parameters, and associations for effective data navigation.

Chapter 8: Data Modeling in the Business Builder

Guide through using the Business Builder tool to create, refine, and enrich data models in this chapter. Learn to create business entities, the foundation of analytical and reporting models sourced from the Data Builder. Cover fact models for simplified consumption model creation with predefined measures, attributes, and filters relevant to specific business contexts. Explore authorization scenarios for filtering data based on user context, concluding with the creation of consumption models vital for data consumption across SAP Analytics Cloud and other BI clients.

Chapter 9: Consuming Data Exposed by SAP Datasphere

Describe data consumption methods within SAP Datasphere, this chapter will educate on consuming data exposed as analytic models, perspectives, and views in SAP and non-SAP tools. Learn multiple data consumption methods, including live connections to SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP's native Add-in for Microsoft Excel.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

I hope you find this book as insightful to read as I found it to write. To learn more and purchase the book, please visit the SAP PRESS website. As you embark on this educational journey, I welcome your thoughts, questions, or feedback. If you find the book valuable, please consider sharing it within your professional network.

Together, we can continue to learn, grow, and innovate in the ever-evolving field of SAP Data and Analytics. Let's connect and keep the conversation going!
